deviations.gdpr_quote: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and that they perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle.
deviations.mismatch: The policy applies the requirement to all AI systems, while the regulation addresses high-risk AI systems specifically.
deviations.analysis: The regulation limits the obligation to high-risk systems; the policy broadens scope to all AI systems, which is over-compliant in terms of applicability. No other contradictions identified; missing elements (accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity specifics) are treated as omissions and thus Non-Deviation.

deviations.gdpr_quote: High-risk AI systems shall comply with the requirements laid down in this Section, taking into account their intended purpose as well as the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI and AI-related technologies. The risk management system referred to in Article 9 shall be taken into account when ensuring compliance with those requirements.
deviations.mismatch: The policy extends applicability beyond high-risk AI systems, whereas the regulation targets high-risk AI systems.
deviations.analysis: Art8(1) applies to high-risk systems and integrates Article 9’s risk management system (REG-003 to REG-012). The policy broadened scope to all AI systems, which is stricter than required (over-compliance). Other elements (state of the art, explicit risk management steps) are not contradicted but simply not present in this chunk; per rules, omissions are Non-Deviation.

deviations.gdpr_quote: High-risk AI systems shall be tested for the purpose of identifying the most appropriate and targeted risk management measures. Testing shall ensure that high-risk AI systems perform consistently for their intended purpose and that they are in compliance with the requirements set out in this Section.
deviations.mismatch: The policy’s applicability is broader than the regulation’s, which requires testing duties specifically for high-risk AI systems.
deviations.analysis: Art9(6) testing duties target high-risk systems, further elaborated by Art9(7)–(10) (e.g., real-world testing, prior-defined metrics). The policy extends scope to all AI systems—over-compliance. No conflicting statements about testing methods or metrics; the remainder are omissions and thus Non-Deviation.
